revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)]][[ja:三和神社]] [[commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)]][[ja:三和神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)]][[ja:三和神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:TchigikenNasugun-Miwajinja-01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nakagawa|WD=Q44603}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.759722|N|140.111944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354664]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine |name = Miwa Shrine |image = [[File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa Town]] Miwa 726 |coordinates = {{coordinates|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miwa Shrine}} |deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto]] |type = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine Listing|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine Listing|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |foundation = [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 12th year ([[:en:604|604]]) |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with copper plate roof |alias = |enshrinement = |festival = [[:en:October 30|October 30]] |rituals = |location_map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Miwa Shrine|caption=|coordinates={{coord2|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (三和神社) is a [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa Town]] Miwa-based [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Old Shrine Rank]] was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:604|604]]) after being enshrined from [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (present-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmi Shrine]], and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of [[:en:Ten'yō|Tenyō]] ([[:en:1144|1144]]), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] ([[:en:1994|1994]]){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Haiden |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa Town]] Miwa 726 |coordinates = {{coordinates|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miwa Shrine}} |deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto]] |type = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine Listing|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine Listing|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |foundation = [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 12th year ([[:en:604|604]]) |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with copper plate roof |alias = |enshrinement = |festival = [[:en:October 30|October 30]] |rituals = |location_map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Miwa Shrine|caption=|coordinates={{coord2|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (三和神社) is a [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa Town]] Miwa-based [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Old Shrine Rank]] was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:604|604]]) after being enshrined from [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (present-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmi Shrine]], and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of [[:en:Ten'yō|Tenyō]] ([[:en:1144|1144]]), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] ([[:en:1994|1994]]){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "pages with conflicting qids" to "qq"
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)]][[ja:三和神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)]][[ja:三和神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)]][[ja:三和神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:TchigikenNasugun-Miwajinja-01.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Nakagawa|WD=Q44603}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.759722|N|140.111944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11354664]] Wikidata name: Miwa-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox shinto shrine |name = Miwa Shrine |image = [[File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), haiden-1.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] |location = [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa Town]] Miwa 726 |coordinates = {{coordinates|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miwa Shrine}} |deity = [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto]] |type = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine Listing|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine Listing|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |foundation = [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 12th year ([[:en:604|604]]) |main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] with copper plate roof |alias = |enshrinement = |festival = [[:en:October 30|October 30]] |rituals = |location_map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Miwa Shrine|caption=|coordinates={{coord2|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (三和神社) is a [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]][[:en:Nasu District, Tochigi|Nasu District]][[:en:Nakagawa, Tochigi|Nakagawa Town]] Miwa-based [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Old Shrine Rank]] was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * [[:en:Ōmononushi|Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto]] == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:604|604]]) after being enshrined from [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]] (present-day [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]) [[:en:Ōmiwa Shrine|Ōmi Shrine]], and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]] as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of [[:en:Ten'yō|Tenyō]] ([[:en:1144|1144]]), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] ([[:en:1994|1994]]){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:qq]]

Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank}} was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |foundation = {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} 12th year ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604}}) |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper plate roof |alias = |enshrinement = |festival = {{ill|October 30|en|October 30|WD=Q2950|lt=October 30}} |rituals = |location_map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Miwa Shrine|caption=|coordinates={{coord2|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (三和神社) is a {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa Town}} Miwa-based {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank}} was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank}} was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - Haiden |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa Town}} Miwa 726 |coordinates = {{coordinates|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miwa Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} |type = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine Listing|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine Listing|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |foundation = {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} 12th year ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604}}) |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper plate roof |alias = |enshrinement = |festival = {{ill|October 30|en|October 30|WD=Q2950|lt=October 30}} |rituals = |location_map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Miwa Shrine|caption=|coordinates={{coord2|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (三和神社) is a {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa Town}} Miwa-based {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank}} was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:pages]]

Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604|1=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō|1=Ten'yō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144|1=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

Haiden |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|WD=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|WD=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa Town|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} Miwa 726 |coordinates = {{coordinates|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miwa Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |type = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine Listing|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine Listing|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (small)

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|WD=Q31048|lt=604|1=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|WD=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|WD=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō|1=Ten'yō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|WD=Q19619|lt=1144|1=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|WD=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

Haiden |religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|qid=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} |location = {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|qid=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|qid=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa Town|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} Miwa 726 |coordinates = {{coordinates|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Miwa Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} |type = {{ill|Engishiki Shrine Listing|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|lt=Shikinaisha|lt_ja=式内社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Shrine Listing|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (small)
Former {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |foundation = {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} 12th year ({{ill|604|en|604|qid=Q31048|lt=604|1=604}}) |main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with copper plate roof |alias = |enshrinement = |festival = {{ill|October 30|en|October 30|qid=Q2950|lt=October 30|1=October 30}} |rituals = |location_map = {{Location map|Japan Tochigi|label=Miwa Shrine|caption=|coordinates={{coord2|36|45|35|N|140|6|43|E|}}|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center}} }} '''Miwa Shrine''' (三和神社) is a {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}{{ill|Nasu District, Tochigi|en|Nasu District, Tochigi|qid=Q1203347|lt=Nasu District|1=Nasu District, Tochigi}}{{ill|Nakagawa, Tochigi|en|Nakagawa, Tochigi|qid=Q44603|lt=Nakagawa Town|1=Nakagawa, Tochigi}} Miwa-based {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former shrine Ranking according to the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Old Shrine Rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gosha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}『栃木県神社誌』，898頁. == deity == * {{ill|Ōmononushi|en|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} == History == According to the shrine legend, the shrine was founded in the 12th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|604|en|604|qid=Q31048|lt=604|1=604}}) after being enshrined from {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}} (present-day {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) {{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|qid=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}『栃木県神社誌』，41頁. {{ill|Fujiwara no Sukeya|ja|藤原資家|lt=Sudo Gon-no-mori Sadamune|lt_ja=須藤権守貞信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Sukeya|1=Fujiwara no Sukeie|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623568}} is said to have prayed for victory when suppressing bandits『栃木県の歴史散歩』，68頁. In the first year of {{ill|Ten'yō|en|Ten'yō|qid=Q1194175|lt=Tenyō|1=Ten'yō}} ({{ill|1144|en|1144|qid=Q19619|lt=1144|1=1144}}), he constructed the shrine’s buildings, and thereafter it was preserved by his descendants, the Nasu clan, until the clan was deposed. After their removal, the shrine has been maintained by its parishioners. The Miwa Ten Festival held at this shrine was designated as a town-designated {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} in the 6th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} ({{ill|1994|en|1994|qid=Q2067|lt=1994|1=1994}}){{Cite web|和書|author=那珂川町|url=https://www.town.tochigi-nakagawa.lg.jp/22kankou/category/jinjya/2009-1217-1720-52.html|title=町指定文化財（民俗文化財）|language=日本語|accessdate=2012-08-30|url-status=|url-status-date=}}. == Shrine Grounds == File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), honden.jpg|Main Hall File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), sandou.jpg|Approach File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), torii.jpg|Torii File:Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi), goukura.jpg|Miwa Gokura == Cultural Properties == === Nakagawa Town-Designated Cultural Property === * {{ill|Folk Cultural Property|ja|民俗文化財|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|lt_ja=無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} '''Miwa Ten Festival''' - designated on July 14, 1994 (Heisei 6). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 『栃木県神社誌』, Tochigi Prefecture Shrine Administration, 2006 * 『栃木県の歴史散歩』, {{ill|Yamakawa Publishing|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Publishing|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11468274}}, 2006, ISBN 978-4-634-24609-6 == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{Shinto Nav}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|三和神社|version=101558556|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:三和神社]] {{Commons category|Miwa-jinja (Nakagawa, Tochigi)}} {{wikidata link|Q11354664}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakagawa Town]] [[Category:Miwa Shrines]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

{{ill|Ōmiwa Shrine|en|Ōmiwa Shrine|qid=Q705542|lt=Ōmi Shrine|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}}, and is identified with the Miwa Shrine of Nasu District in {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}} as listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki
